body:
give to DArren

--- Gmax <gmax1@mindspring.com> wrote:

> Got this e mail from Louella - how would you like to
> handle this
>  
> 
>  Dear Ms. Maxwell
> 
>  
> 
>  
> 
>       Thank you  so much, talking to you was a big
> relief.  Did not
> think Janusz will mentioned it to you, but bless him
> for doing so.
> 
>       
> 
>                  Last October 15, 2005, I co-singed
> a lease to rent a
> two bedroom house with a Filipina I know through
> friends.  The lease is
> one year.
> 
>             On February 21, 2006 while on my way to
> your house in El
> Brillo she called me on my cell and started
> haranging me and to my shock
> 
> 
>             accused of hitting her car, which I
> never did.  She also
> demanded that I moved out.
> 
>                  Since I was concerned that if she
> can accused me of
> hitting her car without any proof, she might be
> capable of doing other
> things, so before 
> 
>             I  decided to leave I called the
> son-in-law of the  owner to
> let him know of what Catalina, my co-renter did.
> 
>             This is now my problem:
> 
>                   The owner thorugh his son Jeff
> Sarmiento asked me to
> pay my share of the rent, since he said Catalina
> refused to sign the
> addendum
> 
>             releasing me from the lease. I am now
> renting an apartment
> with another Filipina that I know for three years
> starting March 1,2006,
> I did this only when Jeff
> 
>             told me Catalina verbally told him that
> she agreed   to
> release me fromthe contract.  He said that Catalina
> changed her tune and
> now they are obligating me 
> 
>             to  pay my share.  I told him it is hard
> for me to pay two
> rents but I do not want my credit destroyed so I
> said yes to him.. I
> even tried to secure a loan
> 
>             but realized later on that it is beyond
> my budget and first
> and foremost Catalina said yes to Jeff.  Ms Maxwell
> I may be naive on
> this lease thing but Ijust want to 
> 
>             do the right thing.
> 
>                    Ms. Maxwell   I do need your
> help.  Do we have a work
> lawyer that you can ask to write a letter of request
> to the owner
> relieving me of my responsibilty
> 
>             to the remaining rent of $590.00 per
> month up to October
> 15,2006.
> 
>                    Anticipating with gratitude for
> your help on this
> problem.
> 
>  
> 
>  
> 
>  
> 
> 
> G

body_html:
give to DArren

--- Gmax gmax1@mindspring.com wrote:

Got this e mail from Louella - how would you like to
handle this

Dear Ms. Maxwell

  Thank you  so much, talking to you was a big

relief. Did not
think Janusz will mentioned it to you, but bless him
for doing so.

             Last October 15, 2005, I co-singed

a lease to rent a
two bedroom house with a Filipina I know through
friends. The lease is
one year.

        On February 21, 2006 while on my way to

your house in El
Brillo she called me on my cell and started
haranging me and to my shock

        accused of hitting her car, which I

never did. She also
demanded that I moved out.

             Since I was concerned that if she

can accused me of
hitting her car without any proof, she might be
capable of doing other
things, so before 

        I  decided to leave I called the

son-in-law of the owner to
let him know of what Catalina, my co-renter did.

        This is now my problem:

              The owner thorugh his son Jeff

Sarmiento asked me to
pay my share of the rent, since he said Catalina
refused to sign the
addendum

        releasing me from the lease. I am now

renting an apartment
with another Filipina that I know for three years
starting March 1,2006,
I did this only when Jeff

        told me Catalina verbally told him that

she agreed to
release me fromthe contract. He said that Catalina
changed her tune and
now they are obligating me 

        to  pay my share.  I told him it is hard

for me to pay two
rents but I do not want my credit destroyed so I
said yes to him.. I
even tried to secure a loan

        but realized later on that it is beyond

my budget and first
and foremost Catalina said yes to Jeff. Ms Maxwell
I may be naive on
this lease thing but Ijust want to 

        do the right thing.

               Ms. Maxwell   I do need your

help. Do we have a work
lawyer that you can ask to write a letter of request
to the owner
relieving me of my responsibilty

        to the remaining rent of $590.00 per

month up to October
15,2006.

               Anticipating with gratitude for

your help on this
problem.

G
